title: anserina muralis
intro: Anserina muralis, also known as sea pygmy-periwinkle, is a low-growing, evergreen plant native to the coastal regions of Western Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Anserina muralis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of infection. Anserina muralis is typically harvested for its leaves and flowers, which can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal preparations for medicinal purposes.
